CSS.AllowedProperties TYPE: lookup/null VERSION: 3.1.0 DEFAULT: NULL --DESCRIPTION-- <p> If HTML Purifier's style attributes set is unsatisfactory for your needs, you can overload it with your own list of tags to allow. Note that this method is subtractive: it does its job by taking away from HTML Purifier usual feature set, so you cannot add an attribute that HTML Purifier never supported in the first place. </p> <p> <strong>Warning:</strong> If another directive conflicts with the elements here, <em>that</em> directive will win and override. </p> --# vim: et sw=4 sts=4
